steps.title: Find the perpendicular slope
steps.content:
From the graph, line $k$ passes through $(0,-5)$ and $(10,3)$. Its slope is
$$
\frac{3-(-5)}{10-0}=\frac{8}{10}=\frac{4}{5}
$$
A line perpendicular to it has slope $-\frac{5}{4}$.

steps.title: Use the given point
steps.content: Line $n$ passes through $(4,7)$, so use point-slope form. $$ y-7=-\frac{5}{4}(x-4) $$ $$ y=-\frac{5}{4}x+12 $$ The answer is B.
